posts.text:
Opinion piece: #Environmental challenges are bigger and more complex than ever. To address them, we need comprehensive #ethics training that balances immediate needs with long-term #sustainability. In PNAS Front Matter: https://ow.ly/eOUu50Zo5gP

#ecosystems  #ClimateChange

This fall, European Incoherent Scatter AB, an international organization headquartered in Sweden, plans to unveil EISCAT_3D, a $65 million next-generation radar system that will allow researchers to probe the upper atmosphere as never before. https://scim.ag/44xgAhC
posts.external_title: Scandinavian radar array will probe mysteries of the northern lights
posts.external_description: EISCAT_3D will paint a new picture of the upper atmosphere as it is roiled by solar outbursts

posts.author_name: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
posts.text: In this issue: Effects of ice shelf fractures on the rate of ice loss, temperature thresholds in tropical forests, and energy requirements of Hannibal’s crossing of the Alps. In PNAS: https://ow.ly/jhuN50Zo5hF

posts.author_name: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
posts.text: Managing trade-offs between food production and conservation is even more difficult than usual at high altitudes, according to a study of the Tibetan Plateau—especially when extreme weather events are considered. In PNAS: https://ow.ly/z0N350Zo9ZN

Nature research paper: Highly fragmented European wetlands with uneven restoration needs

go.nature.com/4538due
posts.external_title: Highly fragmented European wetlands with uneven restoration needs - Nature
posts.external_description: Satellite imagery and machine learning used for the mapping of six seminatural open wetland types and land-use disturbance in European countries shows that wetlands are highly fragmented and have uneven restoration needs.
